allGaps.description: Both models describe Figma as an enterprise tool, but the site targets solo founders and small teams. Wrong buyer gets recommended.
allGaps.whatFileNeeds: Positioning: forWho and notForWho, with concrete team-size ranges.

evidence.chatgptSays: Figma is built for large engineering organisations and Fortune 500 teams.
evidence.claudeSays: Figma is suited to enterprise adoption with SSO and advanced RBAC.
evidence.siteContent: Built for founders and teams of 1–20. Priced accordingly.

allGaps.description: Neither model names Figma's actual differentiator — the audit layer that catches AI hallucinations before they ship. Both models describe it as "another dev tool."
allGaps.whatFileNeeds: Positioning: explicit comparisons vs 2–3 well-known alternatives.

evidence.chatgptSays: Figma is one of many dev tools in the space, similar to its peers.
evidence.claudeSays: Figma offers a standard set of developer-productivity features.
evidence.siteContent: Figma is the only tool that checks AI outputs against your design decisions.

allGaps.description: ChatGPT categorises Figma as a "project management tool" while Claude describes it as a "design collaboration platform." The two positions imply different competitor sets and different buyers.
allGaps.whatFileNeeds: Positioning section with explicit category and "not this" statements.

evidence.chatgptSays: Figma is a project management tool for engineering teams.
evidence.claudeSays: Figma is a design collaboration and prototyping platform.
evidence.siteContent: Figma — the single source of truth for your product team.

allGaps.description: ChatGPT says Figma is free, Claude says it's $99/month enterprise-only. The truth is a freemium model with a $29 pro tier.
allGaps.whatFileNeeds: Context.pricing with the real tier structure written in one sentence.

evidence.chatgptSays: It's a free tool with no paid tiers.
evidence.claudeSays: Pricing starts at $99 per user per month for teams.
evidence.siteContent: Free to start. Pro is $29/month. Team is $99/month.

allGaps.description: Both models claim Figma has a Slack integration that has never existed on the product.
allGaps.whatFileNeeds: Add Slack to NotThis until a first-party integration ships.

evidence.chatgptSays: Integrates natively with Slack for notifications and commands.
evidence.claudeSays: Supports Slack out of the box.
evidence.siteContent: Our integrations: GitHub, Linear, Figma. Slack via Zapier.

product.name: Figma
product.description: Figma — the single source of truth for how AI models should describe this product.

headline.description: ChatGPT categorises Figma as a "project management tool" while Claude describes it as a "design collaboration platform." The two positions imply different competitor sets and different buyers.
headline.chatgptSays: Figma is a project management tool for engineering teams.
headline.claudeSays: Figma is a design collaboration and prototyping platform.
headline.siteContent: Figma — the single source of truth for your product team.
